lede: A look at March 17 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Pietro Pellegri and Fraser Forster, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Rojava Revolution
events.desc: Rojava conflict: At a conference in Rmelan, the Movement for a Democratic Society declares the establishment of the Democratic Federation of Northern Syria.

events.title: 2004 unrest in Kosovo
events.desc: Unrest in Kosovo: More than 22 are killed and 200 wounded. Thirty-five Serbian Orthodox shrines in Kosovo and two mosques in Serbia are destroyed.

events.title: Eritrean War of Independence
events.desc: Eritrean War of Independence: The Nadew Command, an Ethiopian army corps in Eritrea, is attacked on three sides by military units of the Eritrean People's Liberation Front in the opening action of the Battle of Afabet.

events.title: Penmanshiel Tunnel
events.desc: The Penmanshiel Tunnel collapses during engineering works, killing two workers.

events.title: Mount Agung
events.desc: Mount Agung erupts on Bali killing more than 1,100 people.

events.title: 1957 Cebu Douglas C-47 crash
events.desc: A plane crash in Cebu, Philippines kills Philippine President Ramon Magsaysay and 24 others.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: Holocaust: The first Jews from the Lvov Ghetto are gassed at the Belzec death camp in what is today eastern Poland.

events.title: SS Utopia
events.desc: SS Utopia collides with HMS Anson in the Bay of Gibraltar and sinks, killing 562 of the 880 passengers on board.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The British Army evacuates Boston, ending the Siege of Boston, after George Washington and Henry Knox place artillery in positions overlooking the city.

events.title: Edward the Black Prince
events.desc: Edward the Black Prince is made Duke of Cornwall, the first duchy in England.

births.name: Takeo Miki
births.line: Japanese politician, 41st Prime Minister of Japan (died 1988)

deaths.text: John Hemingway (RAF officer), Irish fighter pilot, last surviving Battle of Britain pilot
John Boland (Irish nationalist politician), Irish tennis player and politician
William II of the Netherlands, Dutch sovereign prince and king
Giuliano de' Medici, Duke of Nemours, Italian nobleman

observances.text: Christian feast day: Alexius of Rome (Eastern Church)
Christian feast day: Joseph of Arimathea (Western Church)
